sn4.20:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying among the Kosalans in a wilderness hut in a Himalayan district.
sn4.20:1.2: Then, as he was alone in seclusion, this train of thought arose in his awareness:
sn4.20:1.3: “Is it possible to exercise rulership without killing or causing others to kill, without dispossessing or causing others to dispossess, without sorrowing or causing others sorrow—righteously?”
sn4.20:2.1: Then Māra, the Evil One, knowing with his awareness the train of thought in the Blessed One’s awareness, went to him and on arrival said to him:
sn4.20:2.2: “Exercise rulership, Blessed One! Exercise rulership, O One Well-Gone!—without killing or causing others to kill, without dispossessing or causing others to dispossess, without sorrowing or causing others sorrow—righteously!”
sn4.20:2.3: “But what do you see in me, Evil One, that you say to me, ‘Exercise rulership, Blessed One!
sn4.20:2.4: Exercise rulership, O One Well-Gone!—without killing or causing others to kill, without dispossessing or causing others to dispossess, without sorrowing or causing others sorrow—righteously!’?”
sn4.20:2.5: “Lord, the Blessed One has developed the four bases of power,1 pursued them, given them a means of transport, given them a grounding, steadied them, consolidated them, and undertaken them well.
sn4.20:2.6: If he wanted to, he could resolve on the Himalayas, king of mountains, as gold, and it would become a mountain of gold.”2
sn4.20:3.1: The Buddha: “The entirety of a mountain of gold,
sn4.20:3.3: Even twice that wouldn’t suffice for one person.
sn4.20:3.4: Knowing this, live evenly, in tune with the contemplative life.
sn4.20:4.1: When you see stress, and from where it comes,
sn4.20:4.2: how can you incline to sensuality?
sn4.20:4.3: Knowing acquisition to be a bond in the world,
sn4.20:5.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “The Blessed One knows me; the One Well-Gone knows me”—vanished right there.
